ddls:
CREATE TABLE "energy_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of energy source (e.g., offshore, onshore, pv) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the energy source */,
  "capacity_mw" REAL /* Maximum capacity of the energy source in MW */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the energy source */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the energy source was installed */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Last maintenance date */,
  "status" TEXT /* Operational status (e.g., 'active', 'under maintenance') */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the site where the energy source is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_storage" (
  "storage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each storage solution */,
  "storage_type" TEXT /* Type of storage (e.g., battery, pumped hydro) */,
  "capacity_mw" REAL /* Total capacity of the storage system in MW */,
  "current_storage_mw" REAL /* Current stored energy in MW */,
  "max_storage_mw" REAL /* Maximum storage capacity in MW */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the storage solution */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the storage solution was installed */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Last maintenance date */,
  "status" TEXT /* Operational status (e.g., 'active', 'under maintenance') */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the site where the storage solution is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("storage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "electricity_demand" (
  "demand_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demand entry */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the site for which the demand is recorded */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the day */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the demand entry */,
  "demand_mw" REAL /* Electricity demand in MW */,
  "forecast_demand" REAL /* Forecasted electricity demand for the hour (in MW) */,
  "actual_demand" REAL /* Actual electricity demand for the hour (in MW) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the demand entry (e.g., 'initial', 'final') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_management" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, manager, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  "site_access" TEXT /* Sites the user has access to (comma-separated list of site IDs) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
